name: Carrier Aggregation Scheduling
description: Sub-sector template for carrier aggregation (CA) scheduling and timing synchronization patents. Covers cross-carrier scheduling, component carrier activation/deactivation, dual connectivity (DC), and timing advance group management.
contextDescription: Carrier aggregation is mandatory for LTE-Advanced and 5G NR high-throughput modes. Every flagship smartphone uses CA to bond multiple carriers (up to 16 in 5G). Cross-carrier scheduling and dynamic CC activation/deactivation are key differentiators. Dual Connectivity (EN-DC, NR-DC) adds further complexity by splitting traffic across LTE and NR simultaneously. Key targets: Qualcomm (modem IP), Apple (baseband), Samsung, MediaTek. CA patents have high SEP density — 3GPP TS 38.213 defines CA procedures.
scoringGuidance: CA is used by every flagship smartphone — 5+ component carriers bonded together.
Cross-carrier scheduling (scheduling one CC from another's PDCCH) is highly standardized.
Dynamic CC activation/deactivation patents affect power consumption and battery life.
Dual Connectivity (EN-DC for NSA 5G, NR-DC for SA) patents have very broad reach.
Timing advance group (TAG) management across CCs is critical for CA operation.

questions.question: What carrier aggregation technique does this patent address? Score 8-10 for cross-carrier scheduling or inter-band CA with novel resource management, 6-7 for dynamic CC activation/deactivation, 4-5 for intra-band CA or basic CA configuration, 2-3 for general multi-carrier concepts, 1 for unrelated.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Cross-carrier scheduling and inter-band CA are the most complex and valuable CA techniques.

questions.question: Does this patent address dual connectivity (EN-DC, NR-DC, NE-DC)? Score 8-10 for novel DC traffic splitting or fast secondary cell group (SCG) management, 5-7 for DC-aware scheduling, 3-4 for multi-RAT concepts, 1-2 for no DC aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: EN-DC is used by virtually all NSA 5G deployments worldwide.

questions.question: Does this patent address timing advance group management, inter-carrier synchronization, or clock recovery across aggregated carriers? Score 8-10 for novel TAG management or timing alignment techniques, 5-7 for synchronization improvements, 3-4 for basic timing considerations, 1-2 for no timing aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Timing synchronization across CCs is mandatory for CA — every aggregated transmission requires it.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP CA/DC procedures (TS 38.213/36.213)? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of standardized CA behavior, 5-7 for enabling standard compliance, 3-4 for standard-adjacent, 1-2 for proprietary.
questions.reasoningPrompt: CA is heavily standardized — identify specific 3GPP procedures and section numbers.

questions.question: Does this patent address power efficiency in carrier aggregation (SCell dormancy, adaptive CC management)? Score 8-10 for novel power-saving CA techniques, 5-7 for CC deactivation optimization, 3-4 for general power awareness, 1-2 for no power aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: CA power consumption is a major smartphone battery concern — power-efficient CA is high-value.

questions.question: How detectable is this CA innovation? Score 8-10 for behavior observable in RRC configuration or DCI (CA activation, cross-carrier scheduling), 5-7 for behavior inferable from throughput or conformance tests, 3-4 for internal processing, 1-2 for algorithm-level only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: CA configuration is visible in RRC signaling — carrier activation/deactivation MAC CEs are detectable.
